metadata.title: Shared UI Component Library (Ionic) + Wire All Products
metadata.summary: Build standard shared Vue components in packages/ui/ using Ionic as the foundation for all authenticated products. Dark/light theme, crawler conversation bubble, ClaudeCodePane. Wire all 8 web products.

summary: Standardize on Ionic for all authenticated products. Build OaiAppShell using IonApp + IonSplitPane + IonMenu. Landing stays plain CSS. Wire all products for consistent mobile-first experience.

phases.label: Build packages/ui/ — Ionic-Based Standard Component Library
phases.description: Rebuild packages/ui/ layout components using Ionic as the foundation. Pure prop-driven, no store dependencies. Every authenticated product uses these.

steps.action: Build theme system: CSS variables for dark/light themes that work WITH Ionic's theming. Override Ionic's CSS custom properties (--ion-background-color, --ion-text-color, --ion-color-primary, etc.) for both themes. Create useTheme composable that toggles data-theme attribute and persists to localStorage. Create base.css with OrchestratorAI brand colors (electric blue/violet accent). Both dark and light themes must look polished with Ionic components.

Build OaiTopNav component using IonToolbar + IonButtons: product logo/name (prop), crawler conversation bubble (CrawlerBubble — mini chat widget calling Forge API port 6200 customer-service agent, no auth required, guest sessionId), theme toggle button (dark/light), user menu (IonPopover with name, org, sign out). Props: productName, productIcon, userName, orgName, showCrawlerBubble, showThemeToggle. Emits: signOut. CrawlerBubble opens an IonModal with chat thread.

Build OaiSidebar component using IonMenu + IonList + IonItem: nav items as prop (array of {label, icon, path, children?, badge?}), collapsible sections using IonAccordionGroup, active item highlighting via vue-router (router-link integration), optional product switcher at bottom (links to other product ports). Props: navItems, productSlug, menuId. Works with IonSplitPane for responsive behavior (sidebar visible on desktop, hamburger on mobile).

Build OaiAppShell component: the master layout every authenticated product uses. Structure: IonApp > IonSplitPane > [OaiSidebar (IonMenu) + IonPage > [IonHeader > OaiTopNav + IonContent > slot + ClaudeCodePane]]. Props: productName, productSlug, navItems, userName, orgName, showClaudePane (default true), showCrawlerBubble (default true), adminApiUrl (default http://localhost:6150). Slot: default (page content). This single component gives every product: responsive sidebar, top nav, crawler bubble, theme toggle, Claude pane, and consistent layout.

Build OaiPageHeader component: standard page header used inside content area. IonToolbar-based with page title, optional subtitle, optional action buttons slot, optional breadcrumbs. Props: title, subtitle, showBreadcrumbs. Slots: actions, breadcrumbs.

Build standard UI primitives that wrap Ionic components with OrchestratorAI styling: OaiCard (IonCard), OaiButton (IonButton with brand variants), OaiInput (IonInput), OaiSelect (IonSelect), OaiSearchBar (IonSearchbar), OaiBadge (IonBadge with status colors), OaiModal (IonModal), OaiTabs (IonSegment + IonSegmentButton), OaiTable (custom — Ionic doesn't have tables, build with CSS grid + theme vars), OaiEmptyState (icon + message + action), OaiLoadingSpinner (IonSpinner), OaiStatusDot (colored dot), OaiToast (IonToast wrapper). All use theme CSS variables.

Update packages/ui/index.ts to export ALL new components, composables, and CSS. Update package.json: add @ionic/vue as peerDependency, update exports map. Create packages/ui/README.md with usage examples showing how a product integrates OaiAppShell.

phases.label: Wire Ionic Products (already have @ionic/vue)
phases.description: Command, Admin, Forge, Compose already use Ionic. Replace their custom shells with OaiAppShell. Easiest batch.

steps.action: Wire Command Web (port 6000): Replace AppShellPage.vue + AppNavigation.vue with OaiAppShell. navItems = product launcher tiles based on entitlements. Import theme CSS from @orchestratorai/ui. Keep entitlements store logic — just feed it into OaiAppShell props. Add @orchestratorai/ui to package.json + vite alias. Build + lint must pass.

Wire Admin Web (port 6101): Replace AdminShell.vue with OaiAppShell. navItems = organizations, users, roles, entitlements, LLM analytics (sub: usage/models/costs), RAG management, agent registry, observability (sub: dashboard/events), system health. Import theme CSS. Build + lint must pass.

Wire Forge Web (port 6201): Replace ForgeShellPage.vue with OaiAppShell. navItems = marketing-swarm, legal-department, cad-agent, risk-dashboard, predictor. ClaudeCodePane already wired — remove manual integration, let OaiAppShell handle it. Import theme CSS. Build + lint must pass.
Wire Compose Web (port 6301): Replace AgentsPage.vue shell wrapper with OaiAppShell. navItems = agents, conversations, runner compose. Import theme CSS. Build + lint must pass.

phases.label: Wire Non-Ionic Products (add @ionic/vue)
phases.description: Pulse and Bridge need @ionic/vue added and their custom shells replaced. Slightly more work but same pattern.

steps.action: Wire Pulse Web (port 6501): Add @ionic/vue + @ionic/core + ionicons to package.json. Add IonicVue plugin to main.ts. Replace Tailwind-based AppShell.vue with OaiAppShell. navItems = dashboard, listeners, workflows, scenarios, stream. Remove Tailwind config (or keep as optional utility — Ionic handles layout). Import theme CSS. Build + lint must pass.

Wire Bridge Web (port 6601): Add @ionic/vue + @ionic/core + ionicons to package.json. Add IonicVue plugin to main.ts. Replace custom AppShell.vue with OaiAppShell. navItems = home, registry, inbound, outbound, security, observability, scenarios, demo, settings. Import theme CSS. Build + lint must pass.

phases.label: Wire Landing Web (plain CSS, no Ionic)
phases.description: Landing stays plain CSS for performance. Only add OaiTopNav in public mode (no sidebar, no auth).

steps.action: Wire Landing Web (port 6400): Keep plain CSS — no Ionic. Replace NavBar.vue with a lightweight version of OaiTopNav that works without Ionic (or create a separate LandingTopNav that shares the same brand CSS variables but uses plain HTML). Keep CustomerServiceWidget (crawler bubble). Add theme toggle. Import brand.css from packages/ui/theme/ for consistent colors. Build + lint must pass.
Build ALL web products: npm run build for each. Fix any import resolution or TypeScript issues. Every product must build clean.
Run all web product tests (vitest): Command, Admin, Forge, Compose, Pulse, Bridge. All must pass. Fix any broken tests caused by component changes.

checkpoints.label: Component Library Complete
checkpoints.description: All shared Ionic-based components built, exported, types resolve

checkpoints.label: Existing Ionic Products Wired
checkpoints.description: Command, Admin, Forge, Compose using OaiAppShell — build + lint pass

checkpoints.label: New Ionic Products Wired
checkpoints.description: Pulse and Bridge converted to Ionic + OaiAppShell — build + lint pass

checkpoints.label: All Products Build + Test
checkpoints.description: Every web product builds clean and all tests pass

execution_strategy.notes: Phase 1 builds the library (sequential). Phase 2a/2b/2c wire products (parallelizable). Phase 3 verifies everything.
